soap:
RE-REVIEW · lance-format/lance#7511 — docs(arrow): add SAFETY comments to lance-arrow unsafe blocks
reviewed cdc25d7f → f2596e9b  (1 commit, 1 file)
domain: rust/lance-arrow

# SOAP

## Subjective
Author (LuciferYang, core) pushed one commit since my last review. The PR's
purpose is documentation/hardening: annotate the five `unsafe` blocks in
`lance-arrow` with `// SAFETY:` invariant chains, gate little-endian at the
crate root with `compile_error!`, add a `debug_assert` on the 2-byte alignment
precondition of `as_slice`, and document the `# Panics`/`# Preconditions`
contract on the float traits. The new commit is the one piece I asked for in my
prior review: turn the project's "every unsafe needs a SAFETY comment" rule into
a mechanically-enforced clippy lint.

## Objective
Delta `cdc25d7f...f2596e9b`, scoped to the PR's own files (0 off-branch files
excluded):

- `rust/lance-arrow/src/lib.rs` (+2/-0): adds
  `#![warn(clippy::undocumented_unsafe_blocks)]` at the crate root, immediately
  after the module docstring and before the `compile_error!` little-endian gate.

Verification against ground truth:
- Lint placement is correct (crate root attribute, ahead of items).
- CI `clippy` job PASSES at head, so the new lint does not fire on the existing
  code — i.e. every in-scope unsafe block already carries a SAFETY comment
  (confirmed by reading bfloat16.rs/deepcopy.rs: 5 documented unsafe blocks; the
  one pre-existing block at bfloat16.rs:102 uses lowercase `// Safety:`, which
  the lint accepts case-insensitively, and is untouched by this PR).
- `format`, `rustdoc`, MSRV, mac/windows/linux-arm builds all PASS.
- One red check: `linux-build` FAILS — but the failure is a rustc INTERNAL
  COMPILER ERROR (ICE) while compiling `lance-index` (lib test), not a defect in
  this PR. The compiler panicked in the trait solver (`check_opaque` /
  `evaluate_obligation`) and emitted a `rustc-ice-*.txt` bug-report note. It is
  unrelated to lance-arrow and is infra/toolchain flake; a re-run should clear it.

## Assessment

### Section A — Conditions ledger (0 of 0 met)
No blocking conditions were reconstructed from my prior review — I left no
`issue(blocking)` comments on this PR. The clippy-lint request from my earlier
review was a non-blocking suggestion, and the author has now addressed it
exactly (see lib.rs delta). Nothing outstanding to verify.

### Section B — Fresh-eyes delta
The only change since last review is the two-line lint addition. It is sound,
well-placed, and consistent with the rest of the crate (clippy is green at
head). No new findings. No regressions introduced by the delta.

The lone red CI check is a rustc ICE in an unrelated crate, not attributable to
this change.

## Plan
RECOMMENDATION: approve
- Section A: nothing outstanding (no conditions).
- Section B: no new concerns; delta is the requested lint and it is correct.
- Non-blocking note for the attending: the red `linux-build` is a rustc ICE in
  `lance-index`, unrelated to this PR — recommend re-running that one job rather
  than treating it as a blocker.

confidence: high · what would make this wrong: if the `linux-build` ICE turned
out to be deterministically triggered by the new crate-root attribute rather
than a transient compiler crash — but it fails in `lance-index` (a different
crate that does not depend on this attribute) and the dedicated `clippy`/`format`
/`rustdoc` jobs on lance-arrow are green, so this is very unlikely.

DRAFTED COMMENTS: none (no blocking findings; approve recommendation).
